topic.name: American Revolution
topic.description: Events leading to and including the American Revolution (1775-1783).

events.title: Boston Massacre
events.description: British soldiers fired into a crowd of colonists in Boston, killing five people. The incident became a rallying point for anti-British sentiment.
events.significance: The Boston Massacre became a powerful propaganda tool for colonial activists and helped unite colonists against British rule.

events.title: Boston Tea Party
events.description: Colonial activists, disguised as Mohawk Indians, dumped 342 chests of British tea into Boston Harbor to protest taxation without representation.
events.significance: The Tea Party provoked harsh British retaliation and helped push the colonies toward independence.

events.title: Battles of Lexington and Concord
events.description: British troops marched to confiscate colonial weapons but were met by armed minutemen. The 'shot heard round the world' began the Revolutionary War.
events.significance: These battles marked the beginning of armed conflict between Britain and the American colonies.

events.title: Declaration of Independence
events.description: The Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence, written primarily by Thomas Jefferson, formally announcing separation from Britain.
events.significance: The Declaration established the philosophical foundation for American democracy and inspired independence movements worldwide.

events.title: Washington Crosses the Delaware
events.description: General George Washington led Continental Army forces across the icy Delaware River on Christmas night for a surprise attack on Hessian forces at Trenton.
events.significance: The victory at Trenton boosted American morale after a series of defeats and helped save the Continental Army.

events.title: Battle of Saratoga
events.description: American forces defeated British General Burgoyne's army in upstate New York, marking a major turning point in the war.
events.significance: The American victory convinced France to enter the war as an ally, providing crucial military and financial support.

events.title: Valley Forge Winter Camp
events.description: The Continental Army endured a brutal winter encampment at Valley Forge, suffering from cold, disease, and lack of supplies.
events.significance: Despite terrible conditions, the army emerged stronger and better trained under Baron von Steuben's leadership.

events.title: France Allies with America
events.description: France formally recognized American independence and signed treaties of alliance and commerce with the United States.
events.significance: French support provided crucial military assistance, including troops, ships, and financing that helped win the war.

events.title: Battle of Yorktown
events.description: Combined American and French forces besieged British General Cornwallis at Yorktown, Virginia, forcing his surrender.
events.significance: Cornwallis's surrender effectively ended major military operations and led to peace negotiations.

events.title: Treaty of Paris
events.description: The Treaty of Paris officially ended the Revolutionary War, with Britain recognizing American independence and establishing borders.
events.significance: The treaty established the United States as an independent nation and set its boundaries from the Atlantic to the Mississippi River.
